summary: The test suite covers all four key requirements from the task description — regex renaming, preview mode, conflict detection, and undo script generation — with dedicated test classes for each. Error handling (null/empty pattern, invalid regex, no-op renames, empty directory) is also well exercised. Edge cases are strong: extension-only changes, multi-capture-group patterns, conflict-in-preview-mode, existing-file collisions, and special characters in filenames. The IFileSystem abstraction with a MockFileSystem is a clean, idiomatic design that keeps tests fully isolated from the real disk, and each test creates its own fresh mock so there's no shared state. The RenameLog on the mock is a nice touch for behavioral verification. Minor deductions: (1) MockFileSystem.WriteAllText only tracks the path, not the content, so Undo_SavesScriptToFile only verifies the file appears in the set rather than validating any script content — the SaveUndoScript path is under-verified compared to GenerateUndoScript. (2) MockFileSystem.GetFiles uses string equality on Path.GetDirectoryName, which is fragile on Windows vs. Unix paths and could cause silent test failures on different platforms. (3) No test exercises mid-rename failure recovery (e.g., what happens if RenameFile throws partway through a batch). (4) The RenameLog is declared but never directly asserted in any test, slightly weakening behavioral confidence. Overall a solid, trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions.
